name: Safe Streets and Roads for All (SS4A)
who_applies: local government / MPO / Tribal government (NOT individuals)
applies_to: data-driven projects that reduce roadway fatalities and serious injuries; Implementation grants require an existing safety Action Plan
note: FY2026 is the fifth and final guaranteed round under the IIJA authorization (expires Sept 30, 2026). The resident's lever: ask whether your corridor is in the city's SS4A Action Plan.

name: Highway Safety Improvement Program (HSIP)
who_applies: state DOT / local agency via the state process
applies_to: data-driven infrastructure safety improvements; the standing backbone independent of SS4A
note: Formula program tied to the state's Strategic Highway Safety Plan. Durable even if SS4A is not reauthorized.

name: State / regional / MPO safety set-asides
applies_to: varies; many regions run their own Vision Zero or safety funds
note: Extensible per city; fill in the relevant local program.

name: City maintenance / work order
applies_to: low-cost recurring fixes: vegetation clearance, repainting, signage
note: For these, the ask is a work order, not a funding application.

name: Signal retiming request
applies_to: LPI and other timing changes that need no construction
note: Often the cheapest, fastest win; no capital funding required.
